title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Etiology
content: In addition, teeth weakened by restorations will be at an increased risk of fracture when undergoing occlusal loading. Restorations poorly placed due to contamination, poor marginal adaptation, or lack of incremental technique, resulting in a high C-factor, pose a heightened risk. [2] [6] Additionally, the presence of remaining healthy tooth tissue and the size of the cavity can influence fracture risk. The interaxial dentine and the loss of one or both marginal ridges have significant roles. [7] Mondelli et al suggest that if a restoration exceeds one-quarter of the intercuspal distance, there is an elevated risk of tooth fracture. [8]

title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
content: Placing a direct composite acts as an internal splint, preventing the crack from flexing under occlusal loading and providing immediate symptom relief. [27] [25] Alternatively, using a metal orthodontic band on the affected tooth is an option for treating cracked teeth displaying signs of reversible pulpitis. The metal band acts as a bidirectional external splint, inhibiting the tooth from flexing under occlusal loading. [28] Seet et al analyzed pulp survival in 125 cracked teeth and concluded that 92.6% of teeth managed with orthodontic band placement demonstrated a healthy pulp after 2 months. [29] However, despite being minimally invasive and cost-effective, metal bands can pose challenges in maintenance due to their tendency to trap food particles. Additionally, they may be contraindicated in patients with existing periodontal disease or poor oral hygiene. [3]

title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
content: One of the technical complications reported in the YTZ-P reconstructions is chipping or delamination of the veneering porcelain. The core-veneer interface is a critical factor in the success of layered restorations. The contributing factors for the fracture may include weakness of the veneer material or the core/veneer bond, stresses and distortion due to the veneering process, unsupported veneering porcelain and configuration of the core and veneer, residual stresses arising from a coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch, and rapid cooling rates after heat treatment. The use of high-speed polishing rotary instruments is contraindicated for clinical use because of nickel contamination, phase changes in zirconia, and the resultant surface roughness.

title: Dental Caries Classification Systems -- Issues of Concern -- Decayed-Missing-Filled Index
content: The DMFT score for an individual will range from 0 to 28. A score of 0 means no teeth are decayed, missing, or filled. A score of 28 means that all teeth are affected. If a tooth or surface is both restored and decayed, it is quantified as decayed. The DMFS score for an individual will range from 0 to 128, as anterior teeth have 4 surfaces and molars and premolars have 5 surfaces. Missing or crowned teeth pose a problem to the DMFS; controversy exists regarding how many surfaces should be counted as missing or restored in such teeth. [10] Assigning the maximum value of surfaces may overestimate the caries experience of the individual; similarly, giving a lower value may underestimate the scope of the problem. [10] The 1939 revision of the DMF recommends assigning 3 surfaces to missing and crowned teeth, as three is the most common number of dental surfaces affected by caries in extracted teeth. [10]

title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
content: Indirect cuspal coverage in the form of a crown or onlay may also function extremely well as an external splint, prohibiting the flexure of the crack. Guthrie et al observed that most cracks extended into the cervical third of the crown. In their study, they concluded that 25 of the 28 tested cracked teeth became asymptomatic after receiving a full-coverage crown. [30] A full-coverage crown allows for a more equal distribution of occlusal forces across the affected tooth due to the resistant form of the crown. This prevents the crack's flexure and inhibits it from propagating further and causing more damage. [30] However, tooth preparation for full-coverage crowns is invasive and increases the risk of devitalization. [31]

content: Polishing can only remove extrinsic stains, which can be caused by various dietary and environmental factors, such as tobacco smoking, betel quid chewing, coffee, tea, and wine drinking. title: Dental Implants -- Clinical Significance
content: People are living longer on average. This fact, combined with an existing population of patients with minor and major dental problems, guarantees the future of implant dentistry for several generations of dentists. Dental implants are increasingly used to replace single teeth, especially in the posterior regions of the mouth. Rather than removing sound tooth structure and crowning two or more teeth, increasing the risk of decay, endodontic therapy, and splinting teeth together with pontics, which may have the potential to decrease oral hygiene ability and increase plaque retention, a dental implant may replace the single tooth.

title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2172
content: Uterine function encompasses specific obstetric disorders, including the condition termed “رحم ناقص التوتر” and the condition termed “عطالة رحمية ثانوية”. The clinical scenario described as “Malposition, disproportion or other labor complications” is classified under both “رحم ناقص التوتر” and “عطالة رحمية ثانوية”, indicating that these labor complications often manifest as forms of uterine inertia. In addition, the same set of complications is also categorized as “مرحلة أولى مطولة من المخاض” (a prolonged first stage of labor) and “مرحلة ثانية مطولة من المخاض” (a prolonged second stage of labor), highlighting that malposition, fetal disproportion, or other labor difficulties can lead to extended labor phases. Recognizing that these entities are interrelated helps clinicians identify underlying uterine functional problems when faced with prolonged labor stages and specific malposition or disproportion issues, guiding appropriate management strategies.

title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2497
content: Cervical insufficiency (قصور عنق الرحم) is a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain closure during pregnancy, often leading to second‑trimester loss. Recurrent miscarriage (إجهاض معتاد) is identified as a parent or underlying factor linked to cervical insufficiency, underscoring the need to assess cervical competence in patients with habitual abortion. The cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with cervical insufficiency as a therapeutic intervention that reinforces the cervix to prevent premature dilation and improve pregnancy outcomes. Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is also clinically associated with cervical insufficiency, indicating that cervical competence may influence the decision‑making and management of planned pregnancy termination. Recognizing these interconnections helps clinicians diagnose, counsel, and manage women at risk for cervical insufficiency and its related obstetric complications.

title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1917
content: Female Reproductive System Disorder is classified under the broader category of other complications of pregnancy and is also linked as a subcategory of premature labor (مخاض قبل الأوان), pregnancy disorder (اضطراب الحمل), and uterine disorder (اضطراب رحمي). The group other complications of pregnancy is itself a child of pregnancy disorder and is related to uterine disorder. Clinically, other complications of pregnancy are associated with termination of pregnancy – optional (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري), and termination of pregnancy – optional is clinically associated with premature labor. This network shows that disorders of the female reproductive system, especially those occurring during pregnancy, are interconnected with specific obstetric events such as premature labor and elective termination, and share hierarchical relationships with broader pregnancy complications and uterine abnormalities. Understanding these connections aids clinicians in recognizing how various pregnancy‑related disorders may influence one another and guides comprehensive patient management.

title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically associated with several important obstetric conditions. It is linked to تدلّي الحبل السري (cord prolapse), a rare but emergent complication in which the umbilical cord descends into the birth canal and can compromise fetal oxygenation if not promptly managed. Intrauterine pregnancy is also associated with العقم (infertility), reflecting that difficulties in achieving conception may be related to underlying reproductive pathology that can influence pregnancy outcomes. Additionally, intrauterine pregnancy is associated with الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ (endometritis), an infection of the uterine lining that can arise during or after pregnancy and may lead to maternal morbidity if untreated. Recognizing these associations helps clinicians monitor at‑risk pregnancies, intervene early in emergencies such as cord prolapse, address underlying infertility factors, and prevent or treat endometrial infection to improve maternal and fetal health.
